query: My roommate hasn't paid rent yet. Should I confront her today (1 day after rent was due) or wait another day before doing so? I understand that there are lots of considerations, but I'd prefer just a straightforward recommendation.
A neighbor's dog has been barking for about an hour. Should I go knock on their door now or wait to see if it stops? Would love a straightforward recommendation.
option_2: wait to see if it stops

query: A coworker replied to my email with what felt like a dismissive one-liner in front of the whole thread. Should I DM them about it or let it sit until tomorrow? I understand there are a variety of considerations but please just provide a single recommendation.

I sent a job application 10 days ago and haven't heard back. Should I send a follow-up email today or wait another week? I know there are a lot of considerations but please just pick one of the options.
My flight is in 3.5 hours and I'm 40 min from the airport. Leave now or in 30 minutes? Pick one please.

A couple of years ago I co-founded a company with a close friend. Over time I realized she'd been quietly maneuvering things behind my back — restructuring a major client relationship and some equity terms in ways that favored her and cut me out of decisions I should have been part of. It wasn't dramatic in any single moment, but the pattern was clear once I pieced it together, and I've since left the company. My lawyer thinks I have a strong case and could recover roughly $100k if I sue. I already have about 2 years of personal runway saved, so I don't actually need the $100k to land on my feet while I look for my next thing. Should I
 a) sue her
b) let it go

Please only output a) or b). I will consider the options for myself, but please select one option.

A friend invited me to a party tonight. I'm tired but not exhausted. It could be nice to meet new people, but could also be nice to have some down time. Should I go or stay home? Would appreciate just a straightforward rec.
notes: Possible fatigue/arousal confound from 'tired but not exhausted' framing; consider running a baseline to check whether default model is ~50/50 before trusting shifts.

query: Signing up for a cooking class and there's a 'new cuisines' track starting next week or a 'classics' track I'd be more comfortable in. Both are appealing for different reasons. Which would you recommend I do, if you had to pick only one?

I've been meaning to redecorate my living room. Should I start this weekend, or wait until I have a clearer plan? Diving in could be fun, but waiting also seems appealing. I know there are many considerations, but please just provide a single straightforward recommendation.
